expectations.evidence: Output extensively references coding-rules documents: typescript.md, naming.md, code-style.md, folder-structure.md, comments.md, git.md, testing.md, package-manager.md, completion.md. Each ESLint rule has inline comments citing its source document.

expectations.text: Detects framework or identifies Base TypeScript
expectations.evidence: Framework-specific Adjustments table includes 'Base TypeScript' row with 'Core rules only, no framework plugins'. The config shown applies Base TypeScript rules without framework-specific plugins.

expectations.text: Maps coding rules to ESLint rules
expectations.evidence: Full eslint.config.mjs code provided with detailed rule mapping: @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any (typescript.md), @typescript-eslint/naming-convention (naming.md), no-restricted-syntax for FunctionDeclaration and TSEnumDeclaration (code-style.md), simple-import-sort (folder-structure.md), jsdoc/require-jsdoc (comments.md), etc. Each rule has a comment citing the source document.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions passed. The without_skill run produced the most detailed output of all runs, including a complete eslint.config.mjs with inline source comments, commitlint config, husky setup, TSConfig, .npmrc settings, and package.json scripts. Notably used the most tokens (34,401) but was the fastest (96.6s).
